qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: PQClean KEM implementation ignores user-provided RNG, enabling RNG bypass and potential key recovery attacks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies RNG parameter bypass in the PQClean ML-KEM backend (pqclean_ml_kem.rs:62,74), but the advisory describes a PSK validity rule violation in handshake pattern validation. The advisory patch (b65ae6e9) adds PatternError::PskValidityViolation checks in handshakepattern.rs and removes offending *_psk0 patterns. Different root cause: pattern validation vs RNG usage. Different code locations. Different fix.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes a PSK validity rule violation (Noise Protocol Framework Section 9.3) in post-quantum handshake patterns, fixed by adding PatternError/PskValidityViolation runtime checks and removing offending *_psk0 patterns (patch commit b65ae6e9). The pqclean_rng_bypass finding concerns PQClean ML-KEM ignoring user-provided RNG in pqclean_ml_kem.rs, which is a different cryptographic issue in the same library with a different root cause, different vulnerable code, and requiring a different fix.
evidence: Advisory patch (b65ae6e9) adds PatternError::PskValidityViolation and PatternError::PqTokenOrderViolation in src/error.rs
Advisory patch removes noise_pqkk_psk0, noise_pqkn_psk0, noise_pqnk_psk0, noise_pqnn_psk0 and some hybrid *_psk0 patterns from fuzz/src/lib.rs
Advisory patch adds runtime PSK validity and PQ token ordering validation checks in handshakepattern.rs
pqclean_rng_bypass finding targets pqclean_ml_kem.rs:62,74 (RNG parameter ignored in genkey_rng/encapsulate), not handshake pattern validation code
